pattern: Bezout's identity: an integer linear combination of a set of integers can produce 1 if and only if the GCD of the entire set is 1.
hints: By Bezout's identity, a linear combination (with integer coefficients, possibly negative) of a set of integers equals 1 if and only if their GCD is 1.
Compute the running GCD of all elements in nums; if it equals 1, return true.
If the GCD is any value greater than 1, it divides every possible linear combination, so 1 is unreachable.

edge_inputs.description: all elements are the same large prime power
GCD=1 found early but remaining elements are large

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(100000, max(1, size))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
